title: Magical Moroccan Semolina Tagine
description: A tangy and flavorful Moroccan dish made with semolina flour, this tagine is a quick-fix solution for a delicious meal, and it is gluten-free friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the semolina flour and spices.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat and saute the onion and garlic until softened.
Add the chicken broth, diced tomatoes, salt, and pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in the lemon juice and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the semolina flour mixture to the skillet and stir to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a baking dish and bake for 20 minutes, or until the top is lightly browned.
Remove the tagine from the oven and garnish with chopped parsley.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
